resources.string: Choose the correct definition for the concept.
Press “Try letter” to try to decrypt the term.
Tap the lines in the correct order.
Choose the fragment that completes the code.
Which keyword is used in Java to declare a class?
In Java, a class is declared using the class keyword.
What is the main entry point method in a Java application?
The main() method is the usual entry point of a Java application.
Which data type is used to store true/false values?
The boolean type stores true or false values.
Which keyword is used to create an object?
In Java, new is used to create an instance of a class.
Which modifier allows a member to be accessed from any class?
public allows a member to be accessed from any class.
Which modifier limits access only to the same class?
private restricts access to the inside of the same class.
Which keyword is used to inherit from a class?
extends is used to indicate that a class inherits from another class.
Which keyword is used to implement an interface?
implements is used when a class implements an interface.
Which data type stores whole numbers?
Which data type stores double-precision decimal numbers?
double is used for decimal numbers with more precision than float.
Which class is commonly used for text strings?
String represents text strings in Java.
Which operator is used to compare equality between primitive values?
Which method is recommended to compare the content of two String values?
equals() compares the content of two objects, such as String.
Which structure repeats code while a condition is true?
while repeats a block while the condition is true.
Which structure is used to choose between multiple cases?
switch allows different blocks to run depending on the evaluated value.
Which keyword ends a method execution by returning a value?
return ends the method and can return a value.
Which keyword exits a loop or switch?
break interrupts a loop or a switch branch.
Which keyword skips to the next loop iteration?
continue skips the rest of the current iteration and continues with the next one.
Which keyword prevents a variable from being reassigned?
final prevents a variable from being reassigned after it is initialized.
Which modifier means that a member belongs to the class and not to a specific instance?
static means that the member belongs to the class.
Which keyword is used to define an interface?
interface is used to declare an interface.
Which keyword allows calling the parent class constructor?
super allows access to members or constructors of the parent class.
Which keyword refers to the current object?
this refers to the current object.
Which block is used to catch exceptions?
catch catches an exception thrown inside a try block.
Which keyword is used to manually throw an exception?
Which keyword indicates that a method may throw an exception?
throws is placed in the method signature to declare possible exceptions.
Which collection stores elements in an ordered list?
ArrayList stores elements in order and allows index access.
HashMap stores data associated with keys.
HashSet does not allow duplicate elements.
Which method is used to add an element to an ArrayList?
add() adds an element to a list such as ArrayList.
Which method returns the size of a list?
size() returns the number of elements in a collection.
Which property is used to get the length of an array?
Arrays in Java use the length property.
Which method is used to get the length of a String?
Which file does the Java compiler generate?
The compiler generates .class files with bytecode.
Which command compiles a Java file from the terminal?
Which command runs a compiled Java class?
Which keyword is used to declare a class that cannot be instantiated directly?
abstract indicates that a class may not be fully implemented and cannot be instantiated directly.
Which keyword prevents a class from being inherited?
A final class cannot be extended.
Which annotation is used when overriding a method?
\@Override indicates that a method overrides another method from a parent class or interface.
Hiding internal data and controlling access
Encapsulation means protecting internal state and accessing it through controlled methods.
Two methods with the same name and different parameters
Overloading allows methods with the same name but different parameters.
Overriding redefines the behavior of an inherited method.
Which type of class can contain abstract methods?
An abstract class can contain abstract methods.
Which method runs automatically when an object is created?
The constructor runs when an instance is created.
What must match between a constructor and its class?
The constructor has the same name as the class.
Which package contains many collections such as ArrayList and HashMap?
java.util contains many utilities and collections.
Which class is commonly used to read text from the console?
Scanner can read input from System.in.
In Java, a class can inherit from several classes at the same time.
Java does not allow multiple inheritance of classes. It only allows implementing multiple interfaces.
The main() method can be the entry point of a Java application.
Correct. main() is usually the entry point of a Java application.
A variable declared as final can be reassigned freely.
final prevents a variable from being reassigned after initialization.
String is a class in Java.
String is a class from the standard library.
private allows an attribute to be accessed from any class.
private restricts access to the same class.
public allows access from other classes.
public allows access from outside the class.
int stores whole numbers, not decimal values.
double is used for decimal values.
boolean can only have true or false.
boolean represents logical values: true or false.
== is always the best way to compare String values.
To compare String content, equals() is used.
equals() compares the content of objects such as String.
equals() compares content if the class implements it correctly.
break interrupts a loop or switch.
continue skips to the next loop iteration.
switch is used to choose between several case branches.
try is used with catch to handle exceptions.
throw declares that a method may throw an exception.
throw throws an exception. throws declares it in the signature.
throws is used in a method signature.
throws declares exceptions that the method may throw.
static indicates that a member belongs to the class.
static belongs to the class and not to a specific instance.
this refers to the current object.
super allows access to the parent class.
super allows calling members or constructors of the superclass.
An interface is declared with the class keyword.
An abstract class can contain abstract methods.
Abstract classes can have abstract methods.
A final class can be inherited.
A final class cannot be extended.
A constructor has the same name as the class.
The constructor is named the same as the class.
A constructor always has void as return type.
Constructors do not have a return type.
Arrays use length to get their size.
String values use size() to get their length.
The java command runs compiled bytecode.
The JVM interprets or compiles bytecode.
Bytecode is usually stored in .class files.
Overloading allows methods with the same name and different parameters.
An interface can be implemented by a class.
A class implements interfaces with implements.
System.out.println() prints text to the console.
println prints text and adds a line break.
println prints to the console; it does not delete files.
Scanner can read data entered by the user.
Scanner can read input from System.in.
java.util contains classes such as ArrayList.
protected allows access from the same package and subclasses.
void means that a method does not return a value.
void is used when a method returns nothing.
return can only be used in methods that return int.
return can be used in methods with different return types.
In Java, class is used to declare a class.
System.out.println() prints text and adds a line break.
int stores whole numbers without quotes.
A boolean uses true or false.
String values are written between double quotes.
The body of an if is written between braces.
Complete the code:\n\nfor (int i = 0; i < 10; i____) { }
while executes a block between braces.
return returns an expression or value.
Complete the code:\n\n____ int add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }
public is a valid access modifier for methods.
An attribute needs an identifier, such as name.
new creates an instance of a class.
Complete the code:\n\npublic class Student ____ Person { }
extends indicates inheritance from a class.
Complete the code:\n\npublic class Car ____ Vehicle { }
extends is used to inherit from a class.
Complete the code:\n\npublic class Dog ____ Animal, Pet { }
implements is used to implement interfaces.
An interface can be declared as public interface.
Complete the code:\n\npublic ____ getAge() { return age; }
If it returns a numeric age, it can be int.
Complete the code:\n\npublic void setAge(int age) { this.age = ____; }
The age parameter is assigned to the this.age attribute.
this allows access to the attribute of the current object.
super() calls the parent class constructor.
catch catches exceptions thrown inside try.
Complete the code:\n\npublic void read() ____ IOException { }
throws declares exceptions in the method signature.
add() adds elements to a list.
size() returns the size of a collection.
String uses length() to get its length.
Complete the code:\n\nHashMap<String, Integer> ages = new ____<>();
put() adds or updates key-value pairs in a Map.
case defines a branch inside switch.
break prevents execution from continuing into other cases.
final prevents the class from being inherited.
A constructor does not have a return type.
Complete the code:\n\nScanner sc = new Scanner(____);
nextLine() reads a line with Scanner.
nextInt() reads an integer with Scanner.
Complete the code:\n\npublic static ____ main(String[] args) { }
main is usually declared with void return type.
Complete the code:\n\npublic static void main(____ args) { }
println is formed with print + ln.
Hiding internal data and controlling access
Encapsulation protects the internal state of a class and exposes controlled access through methods.
A class obtains members and behavior from another class
An object is converted to String
Inheritance allows a child class to reuse and specialize behavior from a parent class.
The same type can behave in different ways
A variable belongs to the class
Polymorphism allows objects from different classes to be handled through a common reference.
Methods with the same name but different parameters
Preventing a class from being inherited
Overloading allows several methods with the same name if their signature changes.
A class that cannot be instantiated directly
A class that is always static
A class that only contains attributes
A class that does not allow methods
An abstract class works as a base and can contain abstract or implemented methods.
String does not exist in Java
In Java, String values use double quotes.
Text is being assigned to an int variable
int must be written with an uppercase letter
The variable cannot be called age
An int stores whole numbers, not text strings.
Assignment is used instead of comparison
For comparison, == is used. With booleans, writing if (active) is even better.
The constructor must not have a return type
void is only used in interfaces
A constructor has no return type. If it has void, it is a normal method.
Find the error:\n\nList<String> names = new List<>();
List is an interface and cannot be instantiated directly
String cannot be used with lists
new is not used with collections
List is an interface. It is usually instantiated with ArrayList, for example new ArrayList<>().
println prints the text inside quotes without showing the quotes.
Since x is int, a numeric addition is performed: 5 + 2 = 7.
When there is a String, the + operator concatenates text.
x++ increments the value of x by one.
Both conditions are true, so the result of the logical AND is true.
Order the lines to create a simple Java class:
The class opens with public class, its members go inside, and finally the closing brace is added.
Order the lines to print a message from main:
First main is declared, then println is executed, and finally the block is closed.
Order the lines of a basic if:
The condition opens the block, the instruction goes inside, and the block closes at the end.
Order the lines of a try/catch:
First comes try, then the risky code, then catch, and inside it the error handling.
Order the lines to iterate over a list:
The for-each opens the block, each element is used inside, and finally the block is closed.
Technical word: creates objects in Java.
new is used to create class instances.
class is the keyword used to declare classes in Java.
extends indicates that a class inherits from another class.
catch catches exceptions produced inside a try block.
Technical word: prevents a class from being inherited.
A final class cannot be extended.
Clue: type that stores true or false.
boolean stores true or false values.
Clue: usual entry point method of a Java application.
main is the typical entry point method in a Java application.
Clue: collection that stores key-value pairs.
Clue: keyword used to declare an interface.
Clue: annotation used when overriding methods.
\@Override indicates that a method overrides another one.
